skill.name: firecrawl-scrape
skill.description: Extract clean markdown from any URL, including JavaScript-rendered SPAs. Use this skill whenever the user provides a URL and wants its content, says 'scrape', 'grab', 'fetch', 'pull', 'get the page', 'extract from this URL', or 'read this webpage'. Handles JS-rendered pages, multiple concurrent URLs, and returns LLM-optimized markdown. Use this instead of WebFetch for any webpage content extraction.
skill.summary: Extract clean markdown from any URL with Firecrawl CLI, handling JavaScript-rendered pages and multiple concurrent requests.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 30 patterns that are all false positives. External command findings are documentation examples for the Firecrawl CLI with properly restricted bash patterns (firecrawl *). Network URLs are example placeholders in documentation, not hardcoded endpoints. Filesystem references are markdown cross-links, not path traversal. The skill legitimately wraps the Firecrawl CLI for web scraping with appropriate command restrictions.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI Wrapper
low_findings.description: The skill executes firecrawl CLI commands via bash. Commands are restricted to 'firecrawl *' and 'npx firecrawl *' patterns, limiting injection risk. URL and option inputs could potentially be crafted to escape intended command structure.

content.user_title: Scrape any URL to clean markdown
content.value_statement: Web pages often contain JavaScript-rendered content that traditional fetchers cannot extract. This skill uses the Firecrawl CLI to scrape any URL and return clean, LLM-optimized markdown.
actual_capabilities: Extract clean markdown from static and JavaScript-rendered web pages
Scrape multiple URLs concurrently with single command
Wait for dynamic content to load before capturing page content
Query page content with natural language questions
Output in multiple formats: markdown, HTML, raw HTML, links, JSON
Strip navigation, footer, and sidebar to extract main content only
limitations: Requires Firecrawl CLI to be installed and configured with API access
Query feature costs 5 additional credits per request
Cannot interact with pages requiring clicks, form submissions, or complex navigation
Rate limited by Firecrawl service concurrency limits

use_cases.title: Extract documentation for analysis
use_cases.description: Scrape technical documentation pages to markdown for summarization, comparison, or integration into project knowledge.

use_cases.title: Monitor competitor pricing pages
use_cases.description: Regularly scrape pricing pages and extract changes to track competitor pricing strategy updates.

use_cases.title: Gather research sources
use_cases.description: Extract clean content from multiple web sources for literature review or content aggregation without manual copy-paste.

prompt_templates.title: Basic URL extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape https://example.com/article and save the markdown to .firecrawl/article.md

prompt_templates.title: Extract main content only
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape https://docs.example.com/api with only main content, excluding navigation and footer elements

prompt_templates.title: Handle JavaScript-rendered page
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape https://app.example.com/dashboard waiting 3 seconds for JavaScript to render before capturing

prompt_templates.title: Multi-URL batch extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape these 5 blog posts concurrently and save each to .firecrawl/ directory: https://blog.example.com/post1, https://blog.example.com/post2, https://blog.example.com/post3

output_examples.output: Clean markdown file containing pricing table, feature comparisons, and plan details extracted from the page, ready for analysis or comparison.
Markdown file with documentation content only, excluding site navigation, sidebar, footer, and other non-essential elements.

best_practices: Scrape to a file first, then use grep or read commands to search - more flexible than using --query which costs 5 credits
Always quote URLs containing special characters like '?' and '&' to prevent shell interpretation
Use the .firecrawl/ directory convention for consistent output organization: .firecrawl/{site}-{path}.md
anti_patterns: Do not use scrape for pages requiring login or authentication - use firecrawl-interact instead
Avoid --query for complex analysis - scrape to file and analyze the full content yourself
Do not scrape the same URL repeatedly in quick succession - respect rate limits and use cached results when available

faq.question: When should I use firecrawl-scrape instead of WebFetch?
faq.answer: Use firecrawl-scrape for JavaScript-rendered pages (SPAs), when you need cleaner markdown output, or when scraping multiple URLs concurrently. WebFetch works for simple static pages.

faq.question: What happens if the page requires clicking or form interaction?
faq.answer: Escalate to firecrawl-interact which can perform clicks, fill forms, handle pagination, and other browser interactions that scrape cannot do.

faq.question: How do I extract content from a page that loads dynamically?
faq.answer: Use the --wait-for option with milliseconds to wait for JavaScript rendering, e.g., --wait-for 3000 waits 3 seconds before capturing content.

faq.question: Can I scrape multiple pages at once?
faq.answer: Yes, provide multiple URLs to a single scrape command. They are scraped concurrently up to your Firecrawl concurrency limit.

faq.question: What formats can I export scraped content to?
faq.answer: Supported formats include markdown, HTML, raw HTML, links, screenshot, and JSON. Use --format to specify one or multiple formats.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Firecrawl API key?
faq.answer: Yes, the Firecrawl CLI must be configured with valid API credentials. The skill executes firecrawl commands which authenticate with the Firecrawl service.
